title: dalbergia arbutifolia subsp. arbutifolia
intro: Dalbergia arbutifolia subsp. arbutifolia, also known as the Brazilian rosewood or Brazilian ebony, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family that is native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Dalbergia arbutifolia subsp. arbutifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
